// 02
LOGISTICS & COMFORT
ðŸš„

Transport

  • Primary: High-Speed Rail (HSR) network
  • D-class "Intelligent" Sleepers — Shanghai â†’ Xi'an
  • Z-class Superior Soft Sleepers — Xi'an â†’ Zhangjiajie
  • 2-berth private luxury, saves hotel costs
ðŸ’³

Payments

  • 100% digital — Alipay & WeChat Pay
  • Linked to Australian bank cards
  • Alipay search: å®å¯æ¢¦å¡ç‰Œ = PokÃ©mon Cards
ðŸŒ

Navigation

  • Baidu Translate for communication
  • Amap for navigation (better than Google in CN)
  • VPN recommended for Google / Instagram
âš ï¸

Hard Exit

EXIT BY SEPT 30

Avoid Golden Week — 1.4B domestic travellers. No exceptions.

// 03
THE RADAR
ðŸš Drone Regs

CAAC registration required >250g. No-fly: Beijing / Shanghai Bund. More freedom in Zhangjiajie & Yangshuo.

ðŸ—£ Language

Zero Mandarin. Rely on Baidu Translate + Amap. Younger locals in major cities understand basic English.

ðŸŽ´ PokÃ©mon TCG

Focus on Simplified Chinese (CS) Exclusive Sets. Shanghai & Chongqing official gyms. Huaqiangbei for imports.

ðŸ‹ï¸ Fitness

High verticality in Zhangjiajie & Chongqing. Leg-day heavy. Frequent massage recovery strongly recommended.

// 04
POKÃ‰MON HUNTING PRIORITY
1
Shanghaiâ†’
Big haul — PokÃ©mon Center + Nanjing Road + Xujiahui
2
Hong Kongâ†’
Collector paradise — JP, EN & CN cards. Sino Centre essential.
3
Shenzhenâ†’
Huaqiangbei. Japanese imports + accessories at good prices.
4
Chongqingâ†’
Casual browse in Jiefangbei while exploring the 3D city.
5
Xi'anâ†’
Quick look near university areas. Low expectations.
6
Zhangjiajieâ†’
Skip TCG entirely. Save legs for the mountains.
BRIEF v4.0 Â· MARCH 2026 Â· MELBOURNE ORIGIN Â· EXIT BEFORE GOLDEN WEEK
China 2026 Trip
function loadBoxImages() { document.querySelectorAll('.hunt-card').forEach(card => { const id = card.dataset.id; const wrap = card.querySelector('.hunt-img-wrap'); if (!wrap) return; if (REAL_IMAGES[id]) { wrap.innerHTML = ''; } else { const svg = BOX_SVGS[id]; if (!svg) return; wrap.innerHTML = svg; const s = wrap.querySelector('svg'); if (s) { s.style.width = '100%'; s.style.height = '100%'; } } }); } document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', loadBoxImages); // ===== CITY DATA ===== const CITIES = { shanghai: { phase:'PHASE I', city:'SHANGHAI', days:'4 DAYS Â· DAYS 1–4', cats: ['food','pokemon','nightlife','arch','drone','day','hotel'], content: { hotel: `
RECOMMENDED STAYS
EAST Shanghai (å®œå¿…æ€)
Design-forward business hotel in Pudong. Clean, modern, excellent city views. ~180–250 AUD/night. 10 min metro to The Bund.
DESIGN
The Lido (Jing'an)
Mid-range boutique in Jing'an. Perfect central location — walking distance to Nanjing Road, metro, and French Concession. ~150–200 AUD/night.
BEST LOCATION
French Concession Airbnb
Lane house apartment in the FC for 2 is often cheaper and more atmospheric than hotels. Book early — fills up. ~120–160 AUD/night total.
VALUE
ðŸ“ BASE STRATEGYStay in Jing'an or French Concession. Both have excellent metro access (Lines 1, 2, 10, 12). Avoid Pudong for your base — it's dead after hours.
`, food: `
MUST EAT
Yang's Dumplings (å°æ¨ç”Ÿç…Ž)
Pan-fried pork soup dumplings (shengjianbao). Crispy base, explosive broth. Queue expected. ~8 RMB per order. Best on Wujiang Road.
MUST
Jia Jia Tang Bao
Best xiao long bao in the city per locals. Pure, unfussy, legit. Arrive before open — queue starts early.
MUST
Lost Heaven (èŠ±é©¬å¤©å ‚)
Yunnan cuisine in a gorgeous colonial building on the Bund. Upscale but worth it — try crossing bridge rice noodles.
UPSCALE
Ippudo (ä¸Šæµ·)
Japanese ramen done properly. Strong tonkotsu. Good recovery meal after long days. Multiple branches.
GOOD
Wujie (æ— ç•Œ)
Upscale vegetarian Buddhist cuisine near Jing'an Temple. Surprisingly excellent.
UNIQUE
85Â°C Bakery
Sea salt coffee + fresh bread. Perfect cheap breakfast. Taiwanese chain, everywhere.
CHEAP
FOOD STREETS
Wujiang Road Snack Street
Dense local snack stalls. Quick cheap lunches. Yang's Dumplings is here.
GOOD
Yongkang Road
Outdoor seating, cold Tsingtao, cheap eats. Very local French Concession crowd. Day & night.
GOOD
`, pokemon: `
âš¡ TIER 1 CITY — MAJOR HAULShanghai is the #1 PokÃ©mon TCG city in mainland China. Budget Â½ to 1 full day. Do your biggest buy here.
STORE LOCATIONS
Baimi Xiangxie Commercial Street ç™¾ç±³é¦™æ¦­å•†ä¸šè¡—
Near People's Square. Best TCG hub in Shanghai — multiple small stores across several floors selling PokÃ©mon, Yu-Gi-Oh, Digimon. Main place locals buy TCG. Cheapest prices in Shanghai. Sealed boxes, promo boxes, Gem Boxes, singles.
CHEAPEST
PokÃ©mon TCG Official Gym — Zhongshan Park
Inside Cloud Nine Shopping Mall. Official PokÃ©mon card store — authentic sealed products, promos, TCG events. Prices are retail (not cheapest) but 100% authentic. Official booster packs, promo cards, PokÃ©mon merch.
OFFICIAL
ZX Creation Mall ZXåˆ›è¶£åœº
Nanjing East Road. 6-floor anime / hobby mall with multiple anime and card shops. PokÃ©mon packs and merch across several floors. Strong hobby scene.
GOOD
Jing'an Joy City
Anime and hobby mall with collectible stores. PokÃ©mon packs, anime merchandise, occasional dedicated card stores. Good for a browse while in Jing'an.
BROWSE

ðŸ’Ž CHASE CARD STRATEGY The 4 Oswaldo KATO Pikachu ARs are your primary targets — buy direct from packs or sealed at Baimi Xiangxie. Gem Pack Vol.2 panoramic IRs are secondhand only (no new packs). Ask shops: æœ‰æ²¡æœ‰å®çŸ³åŒ…å•å¡ (do you have Gem Pack single cards?)

âš ï¸ TOURIST TRAP RULEIf a store charges double these prices — walk out. It's a tourist shop. Baimi Xiangxie has the cheapest prices; official gyms are retail.
ðŸš« AVOID THESE
Fake Card Warning
Shanghai has fake PokÃ©mon cards in street markets. Avoid: random loose packs in markets, cards sold near fake toy markets, and any boxes under $10 AUD. Stick to the stores listed above.
AVOID
ðŸ’Ž PRO TIP — HIGHEST UPSIDE PICKSFocus on these three for Western resale value: (1) Collect 151 Hope Booster Box Â· (2) Collect 151 Surprise Booster Box Â· (3) Any Gem Box version. These are the three products Western collectors are importing heavily.

ðŸ” ALIPAY TIPSearch å®å¯æ¢¦å¡ç‰Œon Alipay to find nearby hobby shops not on English platforms.
`, nightlife: `
VENUES
ROOFTOP
Bar Rouge
Bund rooftop. Best view in Shanghai at night. Expensive but do it once. Smart casual.
UNDERGROUND
The Shelter
Converted bomb shelter. Techno venue. One of Asia's most unique clubs. Dark, loud, real.
CRAFT BEER
Jing'an Brewery
Local craft beer in an industrial space. Perfect starting point before heading deeper.
LATE NIGHT
Eddy's Bar
French Concession institution. Friendly crowd, cold beer, open till 4am. No pretense.
BAR STREETS
Yongkang Road
Micro-bar street in the French Concession. Cold Tsingtao, local crowd, no tourist markup. Perfect first night.
MUST
Xintiandi Bar Strip
Premium lane-house bars. More expensive but high design quality.
GOOD
Julu Road
Late-night street with a string of bars and live music. Best after midnight.
LATE
`, arch: `
ARCHITECTURAL HIGHLIGHTS
1933 Old Millfun (è€åœºåŠ)
Former slaughterhouse turned art space. Brutalist concrete labyrinth with spiral ramps and Escher-like geometry. Extraordinary interior. One of the best buildings in China.
MUST
Shanghai Tower
632m twisted supertall. Observation deck at 120th floor. Best view of the Pudong skyline cluster.
MUST
Jin Mao Tower Atrium
Art deco pagoda supertall. The 56-floor cylindrical atrium void from inside is extraordinary.
GOOD
The Bund Heritage Buildings
52 European colonial buildings along the waterfront. Great for contrast shots against Pudong.
GOOD
M50 Art District
Former mill converted to galleries. Industrial photography paradise.
EXTRA
`, drone: `
âš ï¸ DRONE WARNING — READ FIRSTShanghai has strict no-fly zones. The Bund and entire Huangpu riverfront are prohibited. Violation risks confiscation.
DRONE INTEL
CAAC Registration
Mandatory for drones >250g before any flight in China. Register via CAAC app before leaving Australia.
REQUIRED
Flyable Areas
Suburban parks outside the inner ring, Century Park (check app), some Pudong periphery. Always verify in the CAAC app before every flight.
CHECK APP
Hard No-Fly Zones
The Bund, Yu Garden, Nanjing Road, all major landmarks, within 5km of airports.
AVOID
ðŸ“± REQUIRED APPDownload "æ— äººæœºé£žè¡ŒåŠ©æ‰‹" (UAV Flight Assistant) — real-time NFZ overlays for all of China.
`, day: `
4-DAY ITINERARY
DAY 1 — ARRIVE & ORIENT
AFTERNOONCheck in Jing'an. Metro card + Alipay/WeChat activation.
EVENINGWalk The Bund at dusk — Pudong skyline from the west bank.
NIGHTYongkang Road for beers. Low key, jet lag management.
DAY 2 — TCG + ARCHITECTURE
MORNINGPokÃ©mon Center Shanghai — big haul day. Go early weekday.
MIDDAYXujiahui hobby stores + Yang's Dumplings lunch.
AFTERNOON1933 Old Millfun — spend 1.5–2hrs. Photography paradise.
EVENINGShanghai Tower observation deck at golden hour. Night shots.
DAY 3 — FRENCH CONCESSION + NIGHTLIFE
MORNINGSlow morning. Breakfast at 85Â°C. Wukang Road wander.
AFTERNOONTianzifang + Xintiandi. Street photography. More TCG in Nanjing Road.
NIGHTBar Rouge for one Bund rooftop drink â†’ The Shelter for techno.
DAY 4 — DEPART NIGHT TRAIN
MORNINGM50 Art District for industrial photography.
AFTERNOONMassage recovery session. Pack. Final Jia Jia Tang Bao lunch.
NIGHTD-class Intelligent Sleeper â†’ Xi'an. 2-berth private.
` } }, xian: { phase:'PHASE II', city:"XI'AN", days:'3 DAYS Â· DAYS 5–7', cats: ['food','pokemon','arch','day','hotel'], content: { hotel: `
RECOMMENDED STAYS
Hotels Inside the City Wall
Stay inside or immediately adjacent to the ancient walls for the best access. Sofitel Xi'an on Renmin Square is the premium choice. Bell Tower Hotel is a local classic mid-range.
BEST BASE
Muslim Quarter Guesthouses
Small guesthouses in the alley network near Huimin Street. Atmospheric, cheap (~80 AUD/night), but very basic. Good if you want full immersion.
BUDGET
ðŸ“ TIPStaying inside the wall means walking distance to Bell Tower, Muslim Quarter, and the night markets. Worth paying slightly more to be central.
`, food: `
MUST EAT — XI'AN
Rou Jia Mo (è‚‰å¤¹é¦)
The OG. Slow-braised pork stuffed in flatbread. Xi'an's most iconic street food. ~8–12 RMB. Muslim Quarter — look for the longest queue.
MUST
Biang Biang Noodles (æ²¹æ³¼é¢)
Hand-pulled belt noodles with chili oil, vinegar and garlic. The character "biáng" is one of the most complex Chinese characters. Unforgettable texture.
MUST
Yangrou Paomo (ç¾Šè‚‰æ³¡é¦)
Lamb soup with hand-crumbled flatbread. A Xi'an ritual — you tear the bread yourself. Rich and deeply warming.
GOOD
Liangpi (å‡‰çš®)
Cold spicy rice noodles. Perfect contrast to heavier dishes. ~8 RMB.
CHEAP
`, pokemon: `
ðŸ“Š TIER 5 — LOW EXPECTATIONSXi'an is weak for TCG. Don't build a hunt around it — any finds are a bonus.
WHERE TO CHECK
Century Ginwa Mall (Bell Tower)
Check hobby floors. Possible casual singles or sealed packs. Don't expect much.
BROWSE
Near Shaanxi Normal University
Student area. More likely to have TCG than tourist zones. Use Alipay å®å¯æ¢¦å¡ç‰Œ search to find current shops.
BROWSE
STRATEGYSpend 30 mins max. Save budget and energy for Shanghai, Shenzhen and HK.
`, arch: `
ARCHITECTURAL HIGHLIGHTS
Grand Tang Everbright City (å¤§å”ä¸å¤œåŸŽ)
Neon-ancient fantasy pedestrian street. Tang Dynasty architecture in modern LED. Peak cyberpunk meets ancient China. Best at night — unmissable.
MUST AT NIGHT
Xi'an City Wall (è¥¿å®‰åŸŽå¢™)
1370 AD. 12m high, 15m wide at top. Riding the full perimeter by bike at golden hour is one of China's great experiences.
MUST
Bell Tower & Drum Tower
Ancient towers at the exact centre of the old city. Best photographed at dusk with neon streets below.
GOOD
`, day: `
3-DAY ITINERARY
DAY 5 — ARRIVE + WALL + NIGHT MARKET
MORNINGArrive from night train. Check in, refresh.
AFTERNOONRent bikes at South Gate — ride full city wall loop at golden hour.
EVENINGMuslim Quarter dinner — Rou Jia Mo + Biang Biang + Liangpi.
NIGHTGrand Tang Everbright City — full neon-ancient experience.
DAY 6 — TERRACOTTA WARRIORS
EARLYMetro Line 2 â†’ Tourist Bus. Arrive before 9am crowds.
MIDDAYReturn to city. Yangrou Paomo lunch.
AFTERNOONBell Tower area + quick TCG browse.
NIGHTFoot massage. Recover. Early night.
DAY 7 — DEPART NIGHT TRAIN
MORNINGGreat Mosque in Muslim Quarter (free, beautiful courtyard).
AFTERNOONFinal street food run. Shaanxi History Museum (free, world-class).
NIGHTZ-class Superior Soft Sleeper â†’ Zhangjiajie. Private 2-berth.
` } }, zhangjiajie: { phase:'PHASE III', city:'ZHANGJIAJIE', days:'4 DAYS Â· DAYS 8–11', cats: ['food','drone','day','hotel'], content: { hotel: `
RECOMMENDED STAYS
Wulingyuan Area Hotels
Stay in Wulingyuan town — right at the park entrance. Walking distance to the main gates. Avoid staying in Zhangjiajie City itself (40 min away from the park). ~80–130 AUD/night for decent 3-star.
BEST BASE
Pullman Zhangjiajie
Best quality hotel in the area. Pool and good breakfast. ~200 AUD/night. Justifiable given the physical demands.
PREMIUM
ðŸ“ TIPBook with free cancellation — weather determines your plans here. Mist can shut down key viewpoints for days. Flexibility is key.
`, food: `
EATING IN ZHANGJIAJIE
NOTEFood is functional here, not a destination. Wulingyuan town has basic restaurants. Inside the park it's overpriced. Eat big breakfasts at the hotel.
Tujia Cuisine (Local)
Spicy preserved pork, sour fish, fermented vegetables. Unique to the Tujia minority. Any local restaurant in Wulingyuan town. ~60–80 RMB/person.
TRY
Xiaomi Hotpot (Wulingyuan)
Multiple hotpot spots in town. Good group meal after a hard day of hiking.
GOOD
`, drone: `
DRONE OPPORTUNITY — HIGH POTENTIAL
ðŸš BEST DRONE LOCATION ON THE TRIPMore relaxed rules than cities but CAAC registration still required. The pillar formations from above are extraordinary.
Outside Park Boundaries
Flying inside the national park is restricted. Areas just outside the perimeter — especially elevated viewpoints — may permit flight. Always check UAV Flight Assistant app first.
CHECK APP
Golden Whip Stream Valley
Lower altitude valley with pillar formations. Early morning mist shots could be extraordinary. Check NFZ status.
POTENTIAL
Tianmen Mountain Surrounds
Higher elevation. Check NFZ status carefully. Morning fog shots from elevated positions.
SCOUT
`, day: `
4-DAY ITINERARY
DAY 8 — ARRIVE + REST
MORNINGArrive from night train. Check in Wulingyuan. Get park passes.
AFTERNOONLight walk into forest park edge. Scout shots. Don't overdo Day 1.
EVENINGTujia dinner. Early foot massage. Sleep well.
DAY 9 — NATIONAL FOREST PARK
EARLYEnter park at opening. Avatar Hallelujah Mountain viewpoint before crowds.
MIDDAYTianzi Mountain summit cable car. Cloud-level views.
AFTERNOONFairy Trail boardwalk photography. Scout drone zones outside park.
DAY 10 — TIANMEN MOUNTAIN
MORNINGWorld's longest cable car to Tianmen Mountain top.
MIDDAYGlass cliff walk + Heaven's Gate arch. Photography heavy.
AFTERNOONGrand Canyon glass bridge. Drone session outside park if clear.
DAY 11 — DEPART TO CHONGQING
DAWNSunrise attempt at Golden Whip Stream. Final drone session.
MIDDAYHSR via Changsha â†’ Chongqing.
` } }, chongqing: { phase:'PHASE IV', city:'CHONGQING', days:'4 DAYS Â· DAYS 12–15', cats: ['food','pokemon','nightlife','arch','day','hotel'], content: { hotel: `
RECOMMENDED STAYS
Jiefangbei CBD Hotels
Central to everything. Hyatt Regency Chongqing and Wyndham are solid mid-premium options. ~150–220 AUD/night. Walking distance to Hongya Cave and Raffles City.
BEST BASE
Nanshan District
South of the river — stunning night views of the city skyline from your room. ~130–180 AUD/night. Taxi required to get around.
VIEWS
ðŸ“ TIPChongqing's geography is insane. Confirm your hotel's actual floor-level entrance on Amap before arriving — the "ground floor" on your street might be floor 10 of the building.
`, food: `
MUST EAT — CHONGQING
Chongqing Hotpot
Numbing-spicy beef tallow broth. This is the original Sichuan hotpot. Lao Tian'e and Zhu Laowu are top chains. 80–150 RMB/person. Order the fatty beef, tripe, and brain (if brave).
MUST
Chongqing Noodles (å°é¢)
Spicy noodle broth, 10–15 RMB a bowl. Breakfast staple. Any tiny local shop — point at the bowl and nod.
MUST
Jiuzhaitang Hotpot Row
The density of hotpot restaurants here is absurd. Empty stomach required. Order aggressively.
GOOD
`, pokemon: `
TCG INTEL — TIER 4 CITY
Jiefangbei Shopping Malls
Multiple mall floors. Worth 1–2 hour browse. Casual hobby shops and some sealed packs. CN sets + occasional JP imports.
BROWSE
StarLight 68 Mall
Newer mall with anime/hobby mix. Use Alipay å®å¯æ¢¦å¡ç‰Œ search to find current shops — they rotate often.
CHECK
STRATEGYBudget 1–2 hours max. Bonus stop only — save serious money for Shanghai and HK.
`, nightlife: `
UNDERGROUND NIGHTLIFE
ROOFTOP
Raffles City Sky Bridge
Two towers connected 250m up. Bar at the top. The city view at night is jaw-dropping.
UNDERGROUND
Cave Bar Tunnels
Converted WWII air-raid tunnels. Bars carved into rock. Extraordinarily atmospheric.
NEON STREET
Jiefangbei Night Strip
CBD fully lit neon. Packed bars and restaurants. Great street photography.
RIVERSIDE
Nanbin Road
South bank bar strip with views of the cliff-lit skyline. Good for a slower night.
`, arch: `
ARCHITECTURAL HIGHLIGHTS
Raffles City Chongqing
Moshe Safdie's interconnected twin supertowers with a sky bridge at 250m. One of the most ambitious buildings built this century.
MUST
Liziba Building-Train (Line 2)
The metro line that runs through floors 6–8 of a residential building. Photograph from Liziba Bridge at street level.
ICONIC
Hongya Cave (æ´ªå´–æ´ž)
Multi-tiered stilted building on a cliff face. Lit spectacularly at night. Looks exactly like Spirited Away.
MUST AT NIGHT
`, day: `
4-DAY ITINERARY
DAY 12 — ARRIVE + LIZIBA + CAVES
AFTERNOONCheck in. Ride Line 2 through Liziba Building. Photo from outside.
EVENINGHongya Cave at night — photograph from bridge. Best after 7:30pm.
NIGHTCave bar tunnels for drinks.
DAY 13 — RAFFLES + HOTPOT
MORNINGRaffles City sky bridge — morning light on the skyline.
AFTERNOONJiefangbei TCG browse. 1–2 hours.
EVENINGChongqing hotpot dinner. Order aggressively.
DAY 14 — NANSHAN + NIGHT PHOTOGRAPHY
MORNINGChongqing noodles breakfast at a local shop.
AFTERNOONNanshan observation deck — panoramic city shots.
EVENINGNanbin Road riverside bars + long-exposure night photography.
DAY 15 — DEPART TO YANGSHUO
MORNINGFinal street photography — Chongqing is best in morning mist.
MIDDAYHSR to Guilin â†’ bus to Yangshuo (2.5 hrs).
` } }, yangshuo: { phase:'PHASE V', city:'YANGSHUO', days:'4 DAYS Â· DAYS 16–19', cats: ['food','drone','day','hotel'], content: { hotel: `
RECOMMENDED STAYS
Yangshuo Town Boutique Hotels
Multiple solid mid-range options near West Street. YangShuo Mountain Retreat and Zen Garden Hotel are well regarded. ~120–170 AUD/night. Easy access to everything.
GOOD
Rural Li River Guesthouse
Small guesthouses in the countryside near Xianggong Hill. Wake up inside the karst landscape. Cheap (~80 AUD/night) but requires transport. Great for drone base.
ATMOSPHERIC
ðŸ“ TIPRent an e-bike on arrival (~40 RMB/day). It's the best way to explore the countryside and essential for early morning Xianggong Hill runs.
`, food: `
EATING IN YANGSHUO
Beer Fish (å•¤é…’é±¼)
Yangshuo's local dish — Li River carp cooked in beer and chili. Rich and hearty. Every restaurant on West Street does it. Old Place Restaurant is reliable.
LOCAL DISH
West Street Side Alleys
Touristy strip but the side streets have cheaper, more local options. Go one block off West Street.
GOOD
Guilin Rice Noodles (æ¡‚æž—ç±³ç²‰)
Everywhere in the area. Cheap and great for breakfast. ~10–15 RMB.
BREAKFAST
`, drone: `
TOP DRONE DESTINATION — TRIP HIGHLIGHT
ðŸš PRIME DRONE LOCATIONYangshuo has some of the most visually extraordinary drone footage available in China. Karst formations, river reflections, rice paddies, mist. Rules are more relaxed than cities but CAAC registration still required.
Li River Valley
Fly low along the river in morning mist. Golden hour reflections of karst peaks. Most rural valley is flyable. Best 6–8am before boat traffic.
PRIME
Xianggong Hill Surrounds
Elevated position with panoramic valley views. Fly from surrounding fields outside the viewing platform. Morning mist + karst = extraordinary footage.
PRIORITY
Longji Rice Terraces (side trip)
90 min from Yangshuo. Terraced rice fields on steep karst slopes. Incredible aerial geometry.
SIDE TRIP
`, day: `
4-DAY ITINERARY
DAY 16 — ARRIVE + ORIENT
AFTERNOONArrive from Chongqing. Rent e-bike. Scout Xianggong Hill route.
EVENINGBeer Fish dinner. Early night — 5am alarm next morning.
DAY 17 — XIANGGONG SUNRISE + DRONE
5:00 AMDepart for Xianggong Hill. Enter by 5:30am.
SUNRISELi River mist + karst peaks. Best photography of the entire trip.
MORNINGDrone session in surrounding fields after sunrise.
AFTERNOONNap. Cooking class.
DAY 18 — RIVER + MOON HILL
MORNINGBamboo raft on the Li River. 90 mins floating through karst.
AFTERNOONMoon Hill hike at sunset.
EVENINGMassage + rest. Edit footage.
DAY 19 — DEPART TO SHENZHEN
DAWNFinal drone session if weather good.
MIDDAYBus to Guilin â†’ HSR to Shenzhen (3.5 hrs).
` } }, shenzhen: { phase:'PHASE VI', city:'SHENZHEN', days:'3 DAYS Â· DAYS 20–22', cats: ['food','pokemon','arch','day','hotel'], content: { hotel: `
RECOMMENDED STAYS
Futian CBD Hotels
Central to everything. Grand Hyatt Shenzhen or InterContinental Shenzhen for premium. JW Marriott and Sheraton for solid mid-range. ~150–250 AUD/night. Metro to Huaqiangbei is 10 mins.
BEST BASE
Near Huaqiangbei
Budget options abound around the electronics market. Walk to everything. ~80–120 AUD/night. Not glamorous but maximally convenient for TCG day.
BUDGET
ðŸ“ TIPFutian Checkpoint to Hong Kong is literally a 20-min metro ride. Stay close to the metro — Shenzhen is easy to navigate if you're on a line.
`, food: `
EATING IN SHENZHEN
Cantonese Dim Sum
Shenzhen is essentially a Cantonese city. DimDimSum near Futian is excellent and affordable. ~60 RMB/person. Mandatory breakfast experience.
MUST
Dongmen Old Street
Historic pedestrian food street. Good Cantonese snacks and roast meats. Busy weekends.
GOOD
Huaqiangbei Food Courts
The electronics malls have decent food courts. Functional and cheap for lunch while shopping.
CONVENIENT
`, pokemon: `
TCG INTEL — TIER 2 CITY
Huaqiangbei Electronics Market
Top loaders, sleeves, binders, boxes at a fraction of AU cost. Multiple buildings with hobby and anime floors. Also sealed CN booster boxes and JP imports. Navigate via HUAQIANG NORTH STATION (Line 1).
PRIMARY TARGET
SEG Electronics Market (èµ›æ ¼å¹¿åœº)
Multi-floor building with anime and hobby tenants on upper floors. Strong JP import selection.
GOOD
Dongmen Anime Stores
Cluster near the old street. Use Alipay å®å¯æ¢¦å¡ç‰Œ to find current active shops.
BROWSE
WHAT TO HUNTCN booster boxes Â· JP packs at lower prices Â· Accessories (top loaders, sleeves, binders at fraction of AU cost)
`, arch: `
ARCHITECTURAL HIGHLIGHTS
MOCAUP (Chrome Cloud)
Museum of Contemporary Art & Urban Planning. Chrome exterior that reflects the surrounding skyline like a mirror. One of the most photogenic buildings in China.
MUST
DJI Sky City
Foster + Partners twin towers connected by a sky bridge. DJI HQ. Dramatic glass architecture — the atrium is worth seeing. Buy any last gear here.
GOOD
Ping An Finance Centre
599m — 4th tallest building in the world. The Futian skyline from a distance is extraordinary.
VIEWS
`, day: `
3-DAY ITINERARY
DAY 20 — ARRIVE + HUAQIANGBEI
MORNINGArrive from Yangshuo. Check in Futian.
AFTERNOONHuaqiangbei full afternoon — TCG, accessories, tech shopping.
EVENINGDongmen area for dinner.
DAY 21 — ARCHITECTURE + DJI
MORNINGDim sum breakfast. Non-negotiable.
MIDDAYMOCAUP photography. DJI Sky City visit.
AFTERNOONDJI Flagship Store — gear check, any purchases before HK.
EVENINGPing An Finance Centre skyline shots at dusk.
DAY 22 — DEPART TO HONG KONG
MORNINGFinal Huaqiangbei TCG browse if anything remaining.
MIDDAYMetro â†’ Futian Checkpoint â†’ Hong Kong MTR (20 mins total).
` } }, hongkong: { phase:'PHASE VII — EXIT', city:'HONGKONG', days:'3 DAYS Â· DAYS 23–25 Â· EXIT SEPT 29', cats: ['food','pokemon','nightlife','day','hotel'], content: { hotel: `
RECOMMENDED STAYS
âš ï¸ BOOK EARLY — EXIT DATE CRITICALSept 29 is your hard exit. Flights fill fast. Book hotel + flight well in advance.
Mong Kok (Kowloon)
Stay in Mong Kok for maximum TCG access. City View Hotel or Dorsett Mongkok are solid mid-range options. ~130–190 AUD/night. Walking distance to Sino Centre.
BEST FOR TCG
Tsim Sha Tsui (TST)
More polished area with harbour views. Sheraton TST or Peninsula for premium. Easy ferry to HK Island. ~170–280 AUD/night.
HARBOUR VIEWS
ðŸ“ TIPGet an Octopus Card immediately on arrival. Works on MTR, buses, trams and for payments in 7-Eleven and many stores. No Alipay required here — cards work everywhere.
`, food: `
MUST EAT — HONG KONG
Tim Ho Wan (æ·»å¥½é‹)
Michelin-starred dim sum at affordable prices. The baked BBQ pork buns are life-changing. Queue early or book ahead.
MUST
Yung Kee Restaurant (éžè¨˜é…’å®¶)
HK institution since 1942. Famous roast goose — crispy skin, rich meat. The char siu is equally extraordinary. Go for lunch.
INSTITUTION
Mak's Noodle (éº¥å¥€é›²åžéºµ)
Old-school wonton noodles. The gold standard for HK-style wonton soup. Very cheap, very good. Wellington Street, Central.
CLASSIC
Temple Street Night Market
Open-air hawker stalls. Typhoon shelter crab, steamed seafood, cold Tsing Tao. Authentic HK night market experience.
NIGHT MARKET
`, pokemon: `
ðŸ† TIER 2 — COLLECTOR PARADISEHong Kong is the only stop where Japanese, English AND Chinese cards are available together. Budget significant time.
PRIMARY DISTRICTS
Mong Kok (æ—ºè§’)
Dense cluster of anime stores, TCG shops, and hobby malls. Spend most of your HK TCG time here. Multiple buildings across a few blocks.
PRIORITY DISTRICT
Sino Centre (æ–°éƒ½åŸŽä¸­å¿ƒ)
Multiple floors of anime and card shops. Arguably the single best building for TCG in HK. Every floor has something relevant.
MUST HIT
In's Point (å§‹å‰µä¸­å¿ƒ)
Adjacent to Sino Centre. More TCG and anime floors. Known for graded cards and rare singles.
MUST HIT
Fa Yuen Street (èŠ±åœ’è¡—)
"Sneaker Street" also has hobby shops. Good for Japanese imports and English product.
GOOD
WHAT TO HUNT IN HK
Japanese Promos
Graded Cards (PSA/BGS)
Rare Singles
English Sealed
Japanese Sealed
CN Exclusives
`, nightlife: `
HK NIGHTLIFE — FINAL NIGHTS
WORLD'S HIGHEST BAR
OZONE at Ritz-Carlton
484m up in ICC tower. Insane views. One drink is expensive but the view is unmatched. Do it on the last night.
CLASSIC BAR
Wanch Bar (Wan Chai)
HK pub institution. Live music, cold beer, mixed crowd. Very relaxed final night session.
FREE SHOW
Symphony of Lights
Free nightly light show across the harbour skyline. Watch from TST promenade at 8pm. Photograph the spectacle.
NIGHT MARKET
Temple Street
Open-air hawker stalls, street food, fortune tellers. Best 8–11pm. Last night of the trip material.
`, day: `
3-DAY ITINERARY
DAY 23 — ARRIVE + KOWLOON
AFTERNOONCross from Shenzhen. Check in Mong Kok. Octopus Card sorted.
EVENINGTST harbour promenade — HK Island skyline at dusk.
8:00 PMSymphony of Lights show from the promenade.
NIGHTTemple Street Night Market — typhoon shelter crab + cold Tsing Tao.
DAY 24 — TCG PARADISE
MORNINGTim Ho Wan dim sum. Queue early.
AFTERNOONFull afternoon — Sino Centre â†’ In's Point â†’ Fa Yuen Street. Major final haul.
EVENINGOZONE bar at ICC — one drink, extraordinary final night view.
DAY 25 — SEPT 29 — DEPARTURE
MORNINGMak's Noodle wonton breakfast. Final dim sum if time allows.
MIDDAYAirport Express from Kowloon Station. Check in opens 3hrs early.
EVENINGFlight HK â†’ Melbourne. Mission complete.
` } } }; // ===== PANEL LOGIC ===== let currentCity = null; let currentCat = null; function openCity(cityKey, evt) { const city = CITIES[cityKey]; if (!city) return; currentCity = cityKey; document.getElementById('panel-phase').textContent = city.phase; document.getElementById('panel-city').innerHTML = city.city; document.getElementById('panel-days').textContent = city.days; // Build category filter bar const bar = document.getElementById('cat-filter-bar'); bar.innerHTML = ''; city.cats.forEach((catKey, i) => { const catDef = CATS.find(c => c.key === catKey); if (!catDef) return; const btn = document.createElement('button'); btn.className = 'cat-btn' + (i === 0 ? ' active' : ''); btn.dataset.cat = catKey; btn.innerHTML = `${catDef.label}`; btn.onclick = () => switchCat(cityKey, catKey); bar.appendChild(btn); }); // Show first category currentCat = city.cats[0]; renderCat(city, currentCat); // Mark active row document.querySelectorAll('.phase-row').forEach(r => r.classList.remove('active')); if (evt && evt.currentTarget) evt.currentTarget.classList.add('active'); document.getElementById('detail-overlay').classList.add('open'); document.getElementById('detail-panel').classList.add('open'); document.getElementById('detail-panel').scrollTop = 0; document.body.style.overflow = 'hidden'; } function switchCat(cityKey, catKey) { currentCat = catKey; document.querySelectorAll('.cat-btn').forEach(b => b.classList.remove('active')); const btn = document.querySelector(`.cat-btn[data-cat="${catKey}"]`); if (btn) btn.classList.add('active'); renderCat(CITIES[cityKey], catKey); } function renderCat(city, catKey) { const content = city.content[catKey] || '

No info available for this category.

'; const el = document.getElementById('cat-content'); el.innerHTML = `
${content}
`; loadBoxImages(); loadChaseImages(); } function closePanel() { document.getElementById('detail-overlay').classList.remove('open'); document.getElementById('detail-panel').classList.remove('open'); document.body.style.overflow = ''; document.querySelectorAll('.phase-row').forEach(r => r.classList.remove('active')); } document.addEventListener('keydown', e => { if (e.key === 'Escape') closePanel(); }); // ===== HUNT OVERLAY ===== function openHuntOverlay() { const overlay = document.getElementById('hunt-overlay'); overlay.style.display = 'block'; document.body.style.overflow = 'hidden'; // Populate if empty const content = document.getElementById('hunt-overlay-content'); if (!content.innerHTML.trim()) { // Render Shanghai pokemon tab content renderCat('shanghai', 'pokemon'); const catContent = document.getElementById('cat-content'); if (catContent) { content.innerHTML = catContent.innerHTML; // Re-run image loaders on the new DOM setTimeout(() => { loadBoxImagesIn(content); loadChaseImagesIn(content); }, 50); } } overlay.scrollTop = 0; } function closeHuntOverlay() { document.getElementById('hunt-overlay').style.display = 'none'; document.body.style.overflow = ''; } function loadBoxImagesIn(root) { root.querySelectorAll('[data-box-id]').forEach(el => { const id = el.getAttribute('data-box-id'); if (REAL_IMAGES[id]) { el.src = REAL_IMAGES[id]; } else if (BOX_SVGS[id]) { const blob = new Blob([BOX_SVGS[id]], {type:'image/svg+xml'}); el.src = URL.createObjectURL(blob); } }); } function loadChaseImagesIn(root) { root.querySelectorAll('[data-chase-img]').forEach(el => { const id = el.getAttribute('data-chase-img'); if (CHASE_IMAGES[id]) { el.src = CHASE_IMAGES[id]; } }); // Re-attach toggle handlers root.querySelectorAll('.chase-toggle-btn').forEach(btn => { const id = btn.getAttribute('data-chase-id'); if (!id) return; btn.onclick = () => chaseToggle(id); if (chaseFound[id]) { btn.textContent = '✓'; btn.style.color = '#00e87a'; btn.style.borderColor = '#00e87a'; } }); } // ESC to close hunt overlay document.addEventListener('keydown', function(e){ if (e.key === 'Escape') closeHuntOverlay(); });
